_comment: Regression fixture for issue #41 (episode_step: multi-line proof_term can silently break tactic-block parsing with a misleading error). Run against the REAL Lean/Mathlib toolchain with: cargo run --example playtest -- <db_path> crates/proofsearch-mcp/examples/regression_scripts/issue41_multiline_proof_term.json -- step [repro] (Solve path) and step [module_repro] (SubmitModule path, root theorem) must both reach outcome=kernel_verified (before the fix, both shapes failed with a misleading 'introN failed: no additional binders' error, even though the mathematical content was always correct; [module_repro] specifically regression-tests a real bug an adversarial review caught -- assemble_module's root-theorem rendering originally still called the OLD unfixed indent() directly, missed by the first pass of this fix). step [uniform] is the same theorem via an already-uniformly-indented proof term (Solve path), which worked even before the fix and must keep working.

args.source_problem_text: issue #41 regression: naturally-formatted multi-line proof term (first tactic flush, rest indented)

action.proof_term:
intro a ha b hb
  refine ⟨a - 1, b - 1, 1, by omega, by omega, by norm_num, ?_⟩
  ring

args.source_problem_text: issue #41 regression: control case, already-uniformly-indented proof term

action.proof_term:
intro a ha b hb
  refine ⟨a - 1, b - 1, 1, by omega, by omega, by norm_num, ?_⟩
  ring

args.source_problem_text: issue #41 regression (SubmitModule path): the root theorem's own proof_term is the raw client leaf content assemble_module's root-theorem rendering must normalize too, not just render_theorem's other callers

root_theorem.name: root
root_theorem.proof_term:
intro a ha b hb
  refine ⟨a - 1, b - 1, 1, by omega, by omega, by norm_num, ?_⟩
  ring
